Phase-75.NAME: Admin Users and Devices Unified Inventory
Phase-75.summary: Approved plan for merging admin Users and Devices into one compact Users inventory where each user can expand to show device data and existing device actions.
Phase-75.approved-by-operator: Operator confirmed Phase-72 through Phase-76 plan.

scope.in: Admin Users page, admin Devices page deprecation/removal from navigation, user-row expandable device list, existing device action controls, optional minimal admin API read path if current API cannot provide per-user devices, bounded scroll, and admin smoke coverage.
scope.out: User frontend, deploy/install scripts, nginx, docker-compose, VPN runtime topology, MTProto runtime, tariff catalog, YooKassa, destructive action semantics beyond existing confirmation-safe device controls.

requirements.requirement-1: Admin /users must remain the single "Пользователи" section.
requirements.requirement-2: The old separate /devices section must be removed from admin navigation after devices are accessible through /users.
requirements.requirement-3: Admin must see the existing user list as before.
requirements.requirement-4: Clicking or expanding a user must show that user's devices with the useful information currently present in the Devices section.
requirements.requirement-5: Device action buttons from the old Devices section must remain available in the expanded user device list.
requirements.requirement-6: Dangerous actions must keep explicit confirmation and audit-compatible behavior.
requirements.requirement-7: Expanded devices must use bounded scrolling/pagination so a user with many devices does not make the admin page unwieldy.

steps.step-1: Audit frontend-admin Users, Devices, Layout navigation, admin API methods, M-024 endpoints, and existing device action flows.
steps.step-2: Determine whether existing admin API can fetch device rows by user without backend changes.
steps.step-3: If existing API is enough, implement frontend-only merge using current endpoints and typed admin client methods.
steps.step-4: If existing API is insufficient, add the smallest admin endpoint needed for per-user devices while preserving M-024 confirmation/action contracts.
steps.step-5: Build expandable user rows with compact device sublists and loading/error/empty states.
steps.step-6: Move block/unblock/rotate/revoke or equivalent existing device actions into the expanded device sublist with confirmation-safe handling.
steps.step-7: Remove old Devices navigation and handle direct /devices access through redirect or compatibility route.
steps.step-8: Add bounded scroll/pagination for expanded device lists and verify mobile/tablet readability.
steps.step-9: Update Phase-75 admin smoke and backend tests if an API endpoint is added.
steps.step-10: Run frontend-admin build, relevant backend tests if touched, Phase-54/58/61/62 regressions, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, diff check, and protected deploy/runtime guard.

data-flow.flow-1: Admin opens /users -> frontend loads user list through existing admin API.
data-flow.flow-2: Admin expands a user -> frontend loads or reveals that user's devices -> compact device rows render status, metadata, and action controls.
data-flow.flow-3: Admin triggers device action -> existing confirmation flow -> existing M-024 action endpoint or minimal compatible endpoint -> UI refreshes affected user row.

verification-design.gate-2: Admin smoke must assert one visible Users inventory and no separate Devices navigation item.
verification-design.gate-3: Admin smoke must assert expandable user rows expose device information and existing action markers.
verification-design.gate-4: Backend M-024 tests must pass if any admin API changes are made.
verification-design.gate-5: Redaction and confirmation guards must remain active for device actions and user/device identifiers.

risks.risk-1: Merging pages can hide device emergency actions unless the expanded user detail is fast and obvious.
risks.risk-2: A user with many devices can make the page long unless bounded scroll or pagination is built into the expanded area.
risks.risk-3: Adding an API endpoint could accidentally broaden admin access; it must remain admin-only and reuse existing authorization/audit boundaries.

Phase-75.acceptance: Phase-75 is done when admin Users is the single inventory for users and their devices, old Devices navigation is gone or redirected, device actions remain confirmation-safe, and admin/backend/MyGRACE verification passes.
